summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.
evidence_quote: Evaporator refrigerant saturation temperature: 30 to 55°F at coil suction outlet

summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.

title: Coil Sensible Heat Ratio
summary: Typical range of sensible to total heat ratio for dehumidifying coils in practice.
evidence_quote: The air-side ratio of sensible to total heat removed by dehumidifying coils varies in practice from about 0.6 to 1.0

title: Coil Selection Air Velocity
summary: Typical air face velocity range for selecting dehumidifying coils for comfort applications.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Air face velocity for comfort applications

evidence_quote: Dehumidifying coils for comfort applications are frequently selected in the range of 400 to 500 fpm air face velocity.

title: Coil Airflow Resistance
summary: Comparison of airflow resistance between a dry sensible cooling coil and a wet dehumidifying coil.
evidence_quote: A completely dry coil, removing only sensible heat, offers approximately one-third less resistance to airflow than a dehumidifying coil removing both sensible and latent heat.

title: HTW Control Valve Sizing
summary: Pressure drop requirement for control valve sizing in high-temperature water systems.
evidence_quote: The valve and equipment must be sized to absorb, in the control valve at full flow, not less than half the available pressure difference between supply and return mains where the equipment is served.

title: HTW Generator Safety Controls
summary: Safety control requirement for high-temperature water generators to prevent tube failure.
evidence_quote: HTW generators require a minimum water flow at all times to prevent tube failure.

title: Ceiling Plenum Pressure Issue
summary: Potential problem with ceiling plenum applications in high-rise buildings due to stack effect.
evidence_quote: Some ceiling plenum applications with lay-in panels do not work well where the stack effect of a high-rise building or high-rise elevators creates a negative pressure.

title: Return Air Plenum Temperature
summary: Temperature difference in a return air plenum directly below a roof deck during cooling season.
evidence_quote: The air temperature in a return air plenum directly below a roof deck is usually higher by 3 to 5°F during the air-conditioning season than in a ducted return.

title: Security Filtration Enhancement
summary: Enhanced particulate filtration level recommended for air-handling systems in buildings for security and IAQ.
evidence_quote: Enhanced particulate filtration for air-handling systems to the level of MERV 14 to 16 filters not only tends to reduce circulation of dangerous substances (e.g., anthrax), but also provides better indoor air quality (IAQ).

title: Outdoor Air Intake Location
summary: Recommendation for locating outdoor air intakes to protect against security threats.
evidence_quote: In any building, consideration should be given to protecting outdoor air intakes against insertion of dangerous materials by locating the intakes on the roof or substantially above grade level.
